name: Newtonian inverse-square law down to ~50 micrometers
requirement: Any candidate framework that introduces extra spatial dimensions or new long-range forces must respect laboratory tests of the gravitational inverse-square law at sub-millimeter distances. Yukawa-type deviations of the form V(r) = -G m1 m2/r [1 + alpha exp(-r/lambda)] are excluded for alpha ~ 1 down to lambda ~ 52 micrometers.

regime.description: Sub-millimeter to ~10 micrometer separations between rotating tungsten test masses; torsion-balance and microcantilever experiments.

current_bound.reference: Lee et al., 'New Test of the Gravitational 1/r^2 Law at Separations down to 52 micrometers', PRL 124, 101101 (2020), arXiv:2002.11761

what_it_excludes: Large-extra-dimension scenarios (ADD-style) with two extra dimensions are excluded down to compactification radii below ~30 micrometers, ruling out the original 'gravity becomes strong at the TeV scale via mm-sized extra dimensions' proposals. Forbids generic moduli or radion fields with gravitational-strength couplings and ranges above ~50 micrometers.

parameterization.name: Yukawa parameterization of the gravitational potential. Long-distance modifications use a slightly different fit but the same (alpha, lambda) structure.
parameterization.summary: V(r) = -G m_1 m_2 / r [1 + alpha * exp(-r / lambda)]. The (alpha, lambda) plane summarizes lab tests of gravity at sub-mm to ~10 m scales. Excludes both extra dimensions (large alpha at sub-mm lambda) and light moduli (smaller alpha at meter-scale lambda).

procedural.predicted_observable: Force between two test masses at separation r, computed in the framework. Subtract Newtonian, fit residual to Yukawa form to extract (alpha, lambda).
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: alpha < 1 for lambda > 52 micrometers (Eot-Wash 2020). Tighter bounds at larger lambda: alpha < 10^-4 at lambda ~ 1 cm; alpha < 10^-9 at lambda ~ 1 AU (planetary ephemerides).
framework_inputs: Predict the gravitational potential between two static masses as a function of separation, including all new-field contributions.
Identify the dominant non-Newtonian contribution and fit it to Yukawa form to read off (alpha, lambda).
procedural.notes: ADD-style large extra dimensions parameterize directly: alpha = 8 N/3, lambda = R_compactification, where N is the number of extra dimensions. Fitting against the Eot-Wash exclusion plot is one of the cleanest tests for any framework with new long-range forces.

primary_references.citation: Lee et al. (Eot-Wash), 'New Test of the Gravitational 1/r^2 Law at Separations down to 52 micrometers', PRL 124, 101101 (2020)
Adelberger, Heckel, Nelson, 'Tests of the Gravitational Inverse-Square Law', Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 53, 77 (2003)
